page: Amazon Web Services r5a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores, utilizing the AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core capabilities are more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all registering in the middle 50% average tier. Notably, the processor demonstrates strong performance in the PassMark CPU encryption test, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also consistently perform in the average tier.

The instance exhibits performance trade-offs, particularly in tasks requiring high single-threaded throughput or advanced vector extensions like AVX-512, which are not supported by this CPU generation. For example, text processing and medium-model LLM text generation perform poorly. Conversely, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and average multi-core performance make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and large-scale data processing tasks that do not rely heavily on single-core speed.
description: Amazon Web Services r5a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU performance, average memory bandwidth, and strong encryption capabilities, contrasted by poor single-core performance. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers that benefit from large memory capacities rather than high single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 CPU, delivering average multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring 384 GB of DDR4 RAM and 48 vCPUs for memory-intensive multi-core workloads.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance

Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and multi-threaded workloads
